description_long: This object is a pair of scissors with two symmetrical, circular finger loops and long, slender blades that taper to sharp, pointed tips. The handles are simple rings, likely made of metal, and are joined at a central pivot point, allowing the blades to open and close in a shearing motion. The blades are straight and narrow, designed for precision cutting, and the overall construction appears robust and utilitarian. The surface finish is smooth and likely metallic, with a dark, uniform coloration throughout. There are no visible ergonomic features or padding on the handles, and the finger loops are of equal size, suitable for ambidextrous use. No brand markings or decorative elements are visible on any part of the object.
description: A pair of metal scissors with symmetrical circular handles and long, pointed blades, finished in a dark, smooth surface. The design is simple, utilitarian, and unadorned.
description_view_0: Viewed from above, the scissors display their symmetrical circular handles and the closed, pointed blades meeting at the center.
description_view_1: From this side angle, the long, slender blades are prominent, extending sharply from the handles, emphasizing the pointed tip.
description_view_2: This front-facing view highlights the alignment of the blades and the open, circular handles, showing the scissors' full length.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors view 1, with the blades extending outward and the handles visible in profile.
attribute_handle_type: Symmetrical circular handles, uncoated, ambidextrous, no ergonomic or special grip features.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Central rivet joint, visible, non-adjustable, standard design.
attribute_finger_loop_design: Two equal-sized, round loops with smooth edges, no inserts or contours, accommodates one finger each.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Pointed tips, designed for precision cutting, standard sharp style.
